step_by_step_thinking:
Intraocular metastases typically arise secondarily to systemic cancers. Given that breast cancer is one of the more common types of malignancy seen systemically affecting women, it often presents with ocular involvement as well due to its propensity for hematogenous spread. However, among gynecologic primaries listed here, ovarian cancer has a higher tendency towards distant metastasis including to sites such as the eye.

My Answer: B Option B (Ovary)
Your clinical reasoning demonstrates an understanding of how certain tumors tend to metastasize based on their biology and anatomical proximity. The ovary being both a frequent source of secondary malignant growths and having direct vascular connections makes it a plausible site for intraocular metastatic disease despite not being directly listed under common gynae-oncology primaries like breast or cervix.
